reason: Both answers hit nearly the same core points (workflow depth, proprietary data loops, distribution/trust, vertical specificity/evals, brand) and correctly state model quality was never a durable moat. B slightly better captures the "speed of iteration beats racing the labs" key point by explicitly pairing distribution with shipping speed, and grounds the brand/distribution point with concrete examples (Notion, Cursor) atop commodity models, making it more persuasive. A's extra point on cost/latency engineering is useful but not central to the question and doesn't compensate for its slightly weaker treatment of iteration speed as the operative advantage. The gap is small but B is marginally stronger.
